what: Implement a two_sum function that takes a list of integers and a target integer, returning the indices of the two numbers that sum to target. Exactly one solution exists; cannot reuse the same element.
approach: Brute force: nested loops checking every pair O(n^2) time O(1) space — simple but slow for large inputs. Optimal: single-pass hash map storing each value mapped to its index; for each element check if complement (target - num) exists in the map O(n) time O(n) space — much faster in practice.
notes: Return value is a list of two ints (indices). Order does not matter. The function signature should be def two_sum(nums: list[int], target: int) -> list[int]. Place implementation in solution.py.
